author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon CVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: (Greek cix.)

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That those who are going to carry their case to court should be careful to inform either the bishop of Carthage or the bishop of Rome.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It seemed good that whoever wished to go to court, should give notice in the form which is sent to the Church of the city of Rome, that from thence also he should receive a formed letter to court.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: But if receiving only a formed letter to Rome, and saying nothing about the necessity which he had of going to court, he willed immediately to go thither, let him be cut off from communion.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: But if while at Rome the necessity of going to court suddenly arose, let him state his necessity to the bishop of Rome and let him carry with him a rescript of the same Roman bishop.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: But let the formed letters which are issued by primates and by certain bishops to their own clergy have the date of Easter; but if it be yet uncertain what is the date of Easter of that year, let the preceding Easter’s date be set down, as it is customary to date public acts after the consulship.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It further seemed good that those who were sent as delegates from this glorious council should ask of the most glorious princes whatever they saw would be useful against the Donatists and Pagans, and their superstitions.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It also seemed good to all the bishops that all conciliar letters be signed by your holiness alone.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And they subscribed: I, Aurelius, Bishop of Carthage, have consented to this decree, and having read it, now subscribe my name.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Likewise also the rest of the bishops subscribed.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon CVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Whoever from any necessity was going to court, must declare his intention to the bishop of Carthage and to the bishop of Rome, and receive a letter dimissory, and otherwise he shall be excommunicated.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Whatever shall seem to the legates useful against the Donatists and Greeks, and their superstitions, that shall be sought from the Emperor.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Synod against the pagans and heretics.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In the consulship of those most illustrious men Bassus and Philip, the xvith Calends of July, at Carthage, in the secretarium of the restored basilica.* In this council the bishop Fortunatian received a second appointment as legate against the pagans and heretics.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Item, a council against the pagans and heretics.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In the consulship of those most illustrious men Bassus and Philip, the iii.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Ides of October at Carthage, in the Secretarium of the restored basilica*.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In this council the bishops Restitutus and Florentius received a legation against the pagans and heretics, at the time Severus and Macarius were slain, and on their account the bishops Euodius, Theasius and Victor were put to death.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This canon is Canon xij. of Carthage, a.d.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Of “Formal Letters” see Can.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
